diagrams.description: Social Communication studies how people create, share, and interpret meaning within social contexts. This flowchart maps its analytical framework: the ultimate goal is to understand how communication constructs social reality, identities, and relationships. It integrates core process elements (sender, message, channel, receiver, feedback, noise) with contextual layers (cultural, relational, situational) and levels of analysis (intrapersonal, interpersonal, group, mass, intercultural). Major theoretical lenses—symbolic interactionism, social constructionism, agenda‑setting, cultivation theory, uses and gratifications, and CMM—interpret these dynamics. The synthesis reveals key communication functions (informing, persuading, relationship building, identity negotiation, entertainment) and produces outcomes such as shared meaning, social influence, public opinion formation, and cultural change.
